seo.title: 100 CI/CD for AI Apps resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical CI/CD for AI Apps resources for engineers.

content.intro: Building CI/CD pipelines for AI applications requires moving beyond traditional unit tests to handle non-deterministic LLM outputs, prompt versioning, and high-cost evaluation suites. This resource provides a curated list of tools and strategies to automate the testing, deployment, and monitoring of AI features using modern DevOps practices.

items.title: Promptfoo CLI
items.description: Run test cases against your prompts using a matrix of LLM providers. Integrates into GitHub Actions to fail builds if semantic similarity or assertion checks fail.

items.title: DeepEval
items.description: A Python framework for unit testing LLM outputs. It uses 'LLM-as-a-judge' to score metrics like faithfulness, relevancy, and hallucination during the CI phase.

items.title: Giskard
items.description: An open-source library for detecting vulnerabilities in LLMs, including prompt injection and biases. Use it in CI to scan for regressions in model behavior.

items.title: RAGAS for RAG Pipelines
items.description: Specific metrics for Retrieval Augmented Generation. Automate the evaluation of context precision and recall within your CI pipeline to ensure retrieval quality.

items.title: LangSmith CI Integration
items.description: Use LangSmith to run evaluation datasets automatically on every commit. It provides a dashboard to compare performance across different versions of your application.

items.title: Braintrust Eval
items.description: A high-performance framework for running evals in parallel. Use their CLI to trigger evaluation runs that track performance improvements over time.

items.title: TruLens-Eval
items.description: Provides 'feedback functions' to evaluate LLM apps. Can be integrated into build steps to verify that new prompt versions meet quality thresholds.

items.title: Athina AI
items.description: A platform for automated LLM monitoring and evaluation. Useful for setting up CI gates that prevent the deployment of models with high error rates.

items.title: Continuous Eval by Relari
items.description: Open-source toolkit for modular evaluation of RAG pipelines. Ideal for testing specific components like the retriever or the generator in isolation.

items.title: UpTrain
items.description: Provides automated checks for LLM responses. Use it to ensure that your model's output adheres to specific guidelines or formatting requirements in CI.

items.title: Pezzo
items.description: An open-source prompt management platform. It allows you to decouple prompts from code, enabling instant updates without redeploying the entire application.

items.title: Portkey Prompt Registry
items.description: Version and manage prompts in a centralized registry. Use the SDK to pull specific prompt versions in production while maintaining a history in Git.

items.title: DVC (Data Version Control)
items.description: Manage large model files and datasets alongside your code. Use DVC to version the data used for fine-tuning or few-shot prompting in your CI pipeline.

items.title: Weights & Biases (W&B) Prompts
items.description: Track prompt iterations and visualize results. Integrate with CI to log the performance of each prompt variant during automated testing.

items.title: Humanloop
items.description: A tool for prompt engineering that includes versioning and evaluation. It helps bridge the gap between prompt design and CI/CD deployment workflows.

items.title: LangChain Hub
items.description: A central repository for sharing and versioning LangChain prompts. Use it to pull vetted prompt templates into your build process.

items.title: HoneyHive
items.description: An evaluation and observability platform. Use their CI integration to run regression tests on prompts before they are merged into the main branch.

items.title: Git LFS for Model Weights
items.description: Use Git Large File Storage to manage local model weights or configuration binaries within your repository, ensuring they are synced across CI environments.

items.title: PromptLayer
items.description: A middleware for logging and managing prompts. Use it to version-tag prompts in CI to ensure the correct version is used by the application at runtime.

items.title: YAML-based Prompt Templates
items.description: Standardize prompts as YAML files in your repo. This allows for standard Git diffs and automated validation of variable placeholders in CI scripts.

items.title: Vercel AI SDK
items.description: Streamlines the deployment of AI-powered edge functions. Provides built-in support for streaming responses and automated preview deployments.

items.title: BentoML
items.description: A framework for building and deploying machine learning services. Use it to package models as Docker containers for automated deployment via CI/CD.

items.title: SkyPilot
items.description: Run LLMs on any cloud provider with optimized cost. Use SkyPilot in CI to spin up GPU instances for heavy evaluation jobs or model fine-tuning.

items.title: Kamal for GPU Deployments
items.description: Deploy Dockerized AI applications to bare-metal GPU servers with zero-downtime. Excellent for self-hosting models like Llama 3 using CI pipelines.

items.title: Cloudflare Workers AI
items.description: Deploy AI models directly to the edge. Use the Wrangler CLI in your CI/CD pipeline to automate the deployment of inference functions.

items.title: Truss by Baseten
items.description: An open-source model packaging tool. Use it in CI to ensure your model environment is reproducible and ready for production serving.

items.title: Cog by Replicate
items.description: Standardizes the environment for ML models. Use Cog in your build step to create a production-ready Docker container with all GPU dependencies.

items.title: Terraform for Vector Databases
items.description: Automate the provisioning of Pinecone or Milvus indexes. Ensure your vector database schema and metadata filtering are version-controlled.

items.title: Fly.io GPU Instances
items.description: Easily deploy GPU-backed containers. Use Fly's GitHub Action to automate the deployment of inference servers with attached local storage.

items.title: ArgoCD for Model Mesh
items.description: Implement GitOps for AI models. Use ArgoCD to manage the state of model deployments on Kubernetes, ensuring the live version matches the repo.

pro_tips: Use LLM-as-a-judge for semantic evaluation in CI to catch regressions that traditional string matching misses.
Implement cost-capping in CI by using smaller models like Llama-3-8B or GPT-4o-mini for routine unit tests instead of full-scale models.
Store prompt templates as structured YAML or JSON files to allow for meaningful Git diffs and automated validation during Pull Requests.
Utilize shadow deployments (mirroring traffic) to compare a new model version's performance against production without impacting end-users.
Cache evaluation results for identical prompt-input pairs in CI to significantly reduce API costs during frequent build cycles.
